cards.back: A game played multiple times, where the same players are involved in each stage
A stable state in a repeated game where no player can improve their payoff by unilaterally changing their strategy
A classic example of a repeated game, where two players have a dominant strategy to defect, but the Nash Equilibrium is a cooperative strategy
A state in a repeated game where no player can improve their payoff without making another player worse off
A concept in repeated games, where a player's strategy changes from one stage to another, often resulting in a Nash Equilibrium
